topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged on the laptop screen, a jarring, unwelcome sound in the otherwise quiet living room. Sarah froze, her hand hovering over the mouse. It was a profile picture she didn't recognize, a username that felt deliberately obscure. Her heart began a frantic, uneven rhythm against her ribs. She clicked. The feed was a carefully curated highlight reel of life—trips to places she’d never heard of, laughter that sounded too carefree, and interactions with people she didn't know. A wave of heat washed over her face, tightening her chest until breathing became shallow. She quickly minimized the window, the glow of the screen feeling like a spotlight exposing a raw, hidden truth. When her son, Leo, walked in, whistling, Sarah felt a desperate need to look anywhere but at him, focusing instead on the dust motes dancing in the afternoon sun.

[story 2]

I found the login details tucked deep within an old browser history folder. My fingers trembled as I typed them in, the silence of the house amplifying the sound of my own shallow breaths. What I saw wasn't just a secret; it was an entire parallel existence Leo had built. The images were vibrant, unfiltered moments that contrasted sharply with the careful, polite façade he presented at dinner. A cold knot formed in my stomach, a heavy, suffocating pressure. I stared at the screen, the colors too bright, the joy too unguarded. When Leo finally returned, his casual greeting sounded like an accusation. I quickly closed the tab, my gaze fixed on the floor, wishing the wooden planks would swallow me whole.

[story 3]

The discovery happened while I was tidying Leo’s room, ostensibly looking for misplaced textbooks. Tucked behind a loose magazine, I found a bookmark—a cryptic link. Curiosity, sharp and unwelcome, pulled me toward the device. The content that unfolded was a stream of fleeting moments, a digital diary of a life lived outside the boundaries of our shared home. My cheeks flushed a deep crimson, a burning sensation spreading across my neck. I felt clumsy, suddenly aware of every imperfection in my own presence. I retreated swiftly, pulling the magazine back into place with unnecessary force, the silence of the room now thick with unspoken things, heavy with the weight of what I had just witnessed.
